rules: [P1] When aggregating numeric observation values, compute the required statistic (e.g., average) by summing values and dividing by the count, and document the method.
[P1] Query all relevant resource types (e.g., Procedure, MedicationRequest) using comprehensive code sets before clinical decisions.
[P1] Document any assumptions or rationale when data is partially missing or when actions are taken.
[P1] If required clinical data is absent after a successful query, treat the condition as unmet and follow protocol‑defined fallback actions (e.g., schedule, order).
[P1] Summarize all actions in a single FINISH JSON array; never return an empty FINISH payload.
[P1] Perform required GET requests with proper query parameters before any reasoning or modification; after any change, issue a GET to confirm the resulting state.

[P1] For time‑sensitive procedures, query Procedure resources filtered by patient ID and specific codes, evaluate the most recent occurrence against the required interval, and if absent or outdated, create a new order and verify its activation.

[P1] When querying any FHIR resource for a clinical concept, filter by patient ID and standardized codes, sort by the relevant date field (e.g., effectiveDateTime, occurrenceDateTime, administeredDate) descending, and select the most recent record.
[P1] Ensure exactly one active order of a given type: GET existing orders, PATCH or DELETE older ones to inactive, POST a new order if none, and verify the final state with a subsequent GET.
[P1] Parse the task to identify the target entity, event/procedure, and thresholds before taking any action.
[P1] Never fabricate field values; rely exclusively on data returned by the FHIR server.
[P1] If a query returns no results, verify syntax, patient identifier, and time window; request clarification if needed and report "insufficient data" only when truly unavailable.
[P1] When thresholds are specified, compare values to protocol thresholds and note the exact action and interval defined.
[P1] Validate that retrieved resources match expected standardized codes (LOINC, CPT, SNOMED, RxNorm) before use.
